fields.definition_origin: Goal-Gradient Effect, Hull (1932), formalized for human consumer behavior by Kivetz, Urminsky, & Zheng (2006) 'The Goal-Gradient Hypothesis Resurrected: Purchase Acceleration, Illusionary Goal Progress, and Customer Retention' (Journal of Marketing Research, n=600+ across 4 studies). The 'endowed-progress effect' (head-start punch card converts ~2x vs cold-start) is the paper's signature finding. Multi-step progress bar UX pattern codified by NN/g ('Progress Indicators in Wizards', updated 2022) and Wroblewski's web-form research. Mobile-onboarding step counter (Step N of M) became dominant pattern via 2017-2020 wellness/fitness apps and entrenched in Cal AI's 2023-2024 33-step quiz. Progress-bar-fill timing constraints (must show motion within 200ms) descend from Doherty Threshold (1982 IBM, 400ms decision time) and Nielsen response-time research (1993).
fields.core_mechanism: Progress indicators exploit the goal-gradient effect: motivation to reach a goal increases as the goal nears, creating an accelerating completion ramp in late steps. Three sub-mechanisms: (1) Endowed-progress — starting at 'Step 2 of 8' rather than 'Step 1 of 7' adds a free progress unit, doubling completion in Kivetz's car-wash punch-card experiment. (2) Loss aversion (Kahneman & Tversky 1979) — once 60% complete, abandoning means losing the 60% sunk effort. (3) Cognitive certainty — knowing total step count converts unbounded uncertainty into bounded commitment (managed expectation reduces mid-flow churn). For long onboarding (20+ steps), chunked sub-progress ('Section 2 of 4 / Step 2 of 8') reduces the perceived distance via segmented goal-pursuit (Heath, Larrick & Wu 1999). Bar-fill animation timing matters because immediate visual response confirms input was registered (Doherty Threshold) and the kinetic-energy metaphor of progress visibly closing distance is itself motivationally activating.
fields.neuroscience_correlate: Goal-gradient activation correlates with ventral striatal dopamine release proportional to proximity-to-reward (Ramnani et al. 2004; Knutson et al. 2001 — anticipation of monetary reward). Progress bars serve as proxy reward-cues that recruit the same circuit. Endowed-progress likely engages ownership / endowment circuits in ventromedial PFC (Knutson et al. 2008 endowment effect fMRI). Loss aversion at high-progress states involves amygdala + insula activation. Animation timing within Doherty Threshold (sub-400ms) preserves perception of agency; longer delays trigger frontoparietal attention-detection 'something is wrong' alerts.
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) Kivetz, Urminsky, & Zheng (2006), Study 1, n=300+ car-wash customers: pre-stamped (10-stamp card with 2 'free' stamps) vs cold-start (8-stamp card) yielded 34% vs 19% completion — 79% relative lift from endowed-progress. (2) Kivetz et al. Study 2: online music-rating site, completion accelerated as users approached threshold (replicated goal-gradient). (3) NN/g progress-indicator research (multiple studies 2002-2024): multi-step flows with visible step counter outperform unmarked flows on completion; specific lift varies 10-30% depending on flow length. (4) Mobbin teardowns 2024-2026 of Cal AI, Flo, Noom, Headspace, Headway: 100% adoption of step-counter or progress bar; long flows (20+ steps) universally use chunked sub-progress. (5) Adapty 2026: top-decile onboarding flows show progress consistently; bottom-decile often hide it (interpretation: hiding progress correlates with longer perceived flows and abandonment). (6) Nielsen 1993 response-time research: <100ms feels instantaneous, <1s preserves flow, >10s loses attention — bar fill must move within these envelopes. (7) Doherty Threshold (Doherty & Thadhani 1982, IBM) — productivity drops sharply when system response exceeds 400ms. (8) Heath, Larrick & Wu (1999) goal-systems research: sub-goals improve persistence in long tasks (foundational support for chunked sub-progress).
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: Step-by-step completion rate (especially the slope from step ~50% onwards — goal-gradient signature), total onboarding completion rate, time-on-step distribution (faster-toward-end is goal-gradient confirmation), abandonment heatmap by step, perception-of-length user-research code (asked 'how long did that feel?').
fields.evidence_strength: peer_reviewed (Kivetz, Hull, Heath foundations) + industry_benchmark (NN/g, Mobbin, Adapty applied)
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Kivetz 2006: +79% completion from endowed-progress (car-wash punch-card RCT). Industry-applied to mobile onboarding: NN/g reports 10-30% lift from visible progress vs hidden; specific step-counter format effects under-studied. Top-quartile vs bottom-quartile gap (Adapty 2026) is broader (~2x paywall reach) but confounded with other variables.
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) ALWAYS show a progress signal on every onboarding screen. Two formats acceptable: (a) numeric step counter ('3 of 12') — most explicit, easiest to implement; (b) progress bar with subtle fill animation — more visceral, reads modern. Combine both for long flows ('3 of 12' + filled bar). (2) ENDOWED PROGRESS: start the user at step 2 (or further), not step 1. Implementation: total_steps = N+2, visible_step = step_index + 2, OR show pre-filled progress on step 1 ('1 of 8' with 25% bar pre-filled). (3) BAR-FILL TIMING: animate within 200ms of input (Doherty Threshold). React Native: Reanimated 3 withSpring or withTiming, duration 250-400ms; CSS web: transition: width 250ms ease-out. NEVER instantly snap the bar — the kinetic confirmation is the dopamine cue. (4) PROPORTIONAL FILL for long flows — bar fill must be proportional to step (1/12 per step), NOT equal-width gimmick where Step 11 of 12 jumps 50% while early steps creep. Chunked sub-progress for >15 steps: 'Section 2 of 4 / Step 3 of 8' — sub-progress resets per section, top-bar shows chunked section progress. (5) FORBID indeterminate spinners during transitions — user perceives stalled progress (Wasted Loading antipattern). (6) PROGRESS-BACK behavior: when user navigates back, progress should DEcrement (honesty) not stay frozen — frozen-during-back reads as broken. (7) STEP-INDEX PERSISTENCE: store progress state with version, so when content changes (steps added/removed) returning users don't see weirdly-out-of-bounds step counters (item 32 fault tolerance). (8) Visual treatment varies: thin 2-3px bar at top of screen (Cal AI, Flo), full-width 4px below header (Headspace), discrete dot/segment per step (Mau Baron Prayer Lock — 8 dots fill in across top), section-chunked top bar (Noom 30+ step flow). (9) Color: do NOT use indigo-500 default — use brand accent or explicit warm color. Anti-slop: vary the bar color treatment (gradient permitted only if brand-justified, not as decoration). (10) Forbid: percentage-only ('67% complete') without step context — ambiguous. Forbid: progress that visibly pauses or stutters mid-flow.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) Cal AI: thin top progress bar (~3px), proportional fill, smooth Reanimated easing, ember/orange brand color. Step counter NOT shown numerically — bar alone. 33 steps total. (2) Flo period-tracker: top bar with dot-per-step indicator, current step highlighted; sub-progress within sections (cycle profile / health / preferences). (3) Headspace 2024 onboarding: numeric counter '2 of 9' subtle in top-right corner, no bar — dialed-back to align with Sage/Caregiver archetype calm. (4) Noom 30+ step quiz: chunked sub-progress 'Section 2 of 4: Lifestyle' with under-section step counter '3 of 8' — handles the long flow gracefully. (5) Mau Baron Prayer Lock: 8 dots across top, current dot pulses softly (lover/caregiver archetype warmth). (6) Headway book-summary onboarding: thin bar + numeric counter '4/9' bottom-anchored. (7) Endowed-progress example: Lasta (BetterMe-stable fasting app) starts at 'Step 1 of 12' but bar pre-fills 16% on first display — implicit endowment. (8) Counter-example failure: Periscope-era apps that showed 'Step 1 of 1' on every screen because total was unknown — read as broken. (9) Counter-example: Lifesum (older versions) showed indeterminate spinner between steps — NN/g flagged as Wasted Loading.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary; thumb-zone constraints make the top-of-screen progress bar dominant pattern. Web onboarding can use sidebar progress with named steps ('Account / Preferences / Plan / Confirm') — Stripe Atlas, Notion team-creation, Typeform multi-page web flows do this. Web sidebar progress is too space-hungry for mobile vertical. Both share endowed-progress, proportional fill, and Doherty Threshold timing constraints.
fields.platform_design_language_alignment: Aligned with both platform languages. Material 3 Expressive (M3E) introduced richer LinearProgressIndicator and CircularProgressIndicator variants with spring transitions and segment-based visualization (M3E 2025, Android 16). Apple Liquid Glass on iOS 26+ supports translucent progress strips that integrate with the glass material. Both platforms support determinate (preferred for onboarding) vs indeterminate (forbidden mid-step). Brand-coloring the progress bar overrides system tint via tintColor (iOS) / progressBackgroundTintList (Android).
fields.category_fit: Universal across mobile onboarding categories. Strongest leverage in long-flows (>10 steps) where without progress users abandon (wellness, AI-wrapper). Useful but lower-leverage in short flows (4-screen cinematic — item 25 — where progress dots are decorative but harmless). Mandatory for any flow with mandatory KYC steps where users need to see how much paperwork remains. Skip only for ultra-short (1-2 screen) flows where the step-counter would feel patronizing.
fields.solo_dev_feasibility: same_day. Implement progress bar component in 1-2 hours; endowed-progress logic is a 1-line config. Chunked sub-progress for long flows is 1 day of work.
fields.tooling_options: react-native-progress (lightweight), Reanimated 3 SharedValue for fill animation, expo-linear-gradient (only if brand-justified gradient), Rive for celebratory progress (advanced), Plotline / Adapty / Superwall onboarding builders all ship with progress components. Native: SwiftUI ProgressView, Android M3E LinearProgressIndicator. Avoid: HTML <progress> default styling on web — looks generic; restyle.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude with the goal-gradient + endowed-progress mechanics named explicitly. Prompt fragment 1: 'Implement a top-of-screen progress bar for a 12-step onboarding flow. Use Reanimated 3 SharedValue width animation, withTiming duration 280ms easing Easing.out(Easing.cubic). FORBID indeterminate spinner. FORBID instantaneous bar snap. ENDOWED PROGRESS: bar starts pre-filled at 16% on Step 1 — visible_progress = (step_index + 2) / (total_steps + 2). Numeric counter "3 of 12" subtle in top-right Söhne Mono 12px ink/60. Bar height 3px, full-width minus 24dp side margin, brand-color #E36B2C fill on ink/8 track. NO Tailwind indigo-500. NO purple-blue gradient. Bar must visibly animate within 200ms of step transition (Doherty Threshold).' Prompt fragment 2: 'For a 28-step onboarding, implement chunked sub-progress: top bar shows 4-segment section progress (each section 7 steps), within section show sub-counter "3 of 7" in 12pt Söhne Mono. When section advances, the section segment fills with a 0.4s spring animation (damping:14, stiffness:140). Persist {section_index, step_within_section} to MMKV on every transition.' Prompt fragment 3: 'Audit every onboarding screen — is progress visible? Is fill proportional? Does it animate within 200ms? Output an audit table.'
fields.stitch_application: Stitch must specify the visual treatment exactly. Prompt fragment 1: 'Mockup onboarding screen with progress indicator. Top of screen: 3px-tall progress bar, full-width minus 24px horizontal margin, 16px from top safe-area, ink-color/8 #1A1816 at 8% opacity track, ember-orange #E36B2C fill at 33% width (for step 4 of 12 with endowed-progress 2-step head-start). Right of bar, 12px gap: numeric counter "4 of 12" in Söhne Mono 12px ink/60. NO percentage display. NO indigo-500. NO gradient on bar. Below progress: 32px breathing room before question header begins.' Prompt fragment 2: 'For long-flow chunked progress: top of screen shows 4 horizontal segments separated by 4px gaps, each segment 60px wide / 3px tall. Segments 1 fully filled (#E36B2C), segment 2 partially filled (47% of its width), segments 3-4 empty (ink/8). Below: section label "Section 2 of 4: Lifestyle" Söhne 13px ink/60, with sub-counter "Step 3 of 7" Söhne Mono 11px ink/40 right-aligned.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI defaults to indeterminate <ActivityIndicator> spinner during step transitions (Wasted Loading antipattern). AI also defaults to <Progress.Bar> from react-native-progress with stock indigo-500 fill, no endowed-progress, no proportional logic. AI computes percentage as step/total (no head-start). AI uses <ProgressBar style={{flex:1}}/> producing equal-width visual gimmick. Counter-prompt: 'FORBID indeterminate spinners. FORBID react-native-progress default stylings. Implement manually with Reanimated 3 SharedValue. ENDOWED-PROGRESS formula: visible_progress = (current_step + 2) / (total_steps + 2). Use brand color #E36B2C. Bar height 3px (NOT 8px which reads heavy). FORBID Tailwind indigo-500/violet-500/purple-blue gradient on the bar.' AI sometimes hides progress entirely on AI-reveal screen — counter: 'progress reaches 100% at step N-1 (loader screen) with celebratory pulse, then disappears at paywall (step N) — paywall has no progress because it's not part of the questionnaire.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) Indeterminate spinner during step transitions — Wasted Loading. (2) Equal-width segment gimmick where 'Step 11 of 12' is half the bar — destroys trust. (3) Hidden progress on long flows — abandonment doubles. (4) Percentage-only display ('67% complete') without step context — ambiguous, less motivating than discrete count. (5) Endowed-progress applied dishonestly (showing '5 of 8' when only 3 of 6 actual questions) — readers detect, trust craters. (6) Backward navigation that doesn't decrement progress — feels broken. (7) Generic indigo-500 fill — AI-slop tell. (8) Progress that pauses or jumps non-monotonically (e.g., 67% → 50% mid-flow due to revealed sub-steps) — anxiety-inducing. (9) Long flow without chunked sub-progress — '3 of 33' is demotivating; 'Section 1 of 4 / Step 3 of 8' is bearable.
fields.regulatory_risk: Low. Progress indicators are compliance-positive — informing users of flow length aligns with GDPR Article 13 transparency and EU Digital Fairness Act (DFA) draft anti-deceptive-pattern principles. Risk only if: (a) endowed-progress is applied dishonestly to mask required additional steps the user can't see (potential DFA dark-pattern flag); (b) progress bar manipulates speed (bar zooms 0-90% in seconds, then crawls — perceived as urgency manipulation, DFA risk).
fields.platform_constraints_2026: No platform forbids progress indicators. Apple HIG recommends progress feedback in onboarding. Google Play / Apple Guidelines neutral on visual treatment. Apple Liquid Glass material guidelines suggest translucent progress strips in iOS 26+. M3E specifies progress component variants (Wave, Linear, Circular) — implement custom if brand requires. EU DFA Q4 2026 may require accurate progress (no manipulative pacing).
fields.accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2 §4.1.2 Name, Role, Value — progress bar must expose accessibilityRole='progressbar' with accessibilityValue={{min, max, now, text}}. VoiceOver/TalkBack announces 'Step 4 of 12' when focus enters screen. WCAG §2.3.3 Animation from Interactions — bar animation respects prefers-reduced-motion (replace spring with instant snap if user has reduced-motion enabled). Color contrast: progress fill vs track ≥3:1 (WCAG 1.4.11). Numeric counter must NOT be the only signal (some users blind to motion); pair with screen-reader announcement. Keyboard-operable (web): focusable progress bar exposes value to AT.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) Endowed-progress crosses dark-pattern line if dishonest — be transparent. (2) Long flows beyond 33 steps tank completion regardless of progress treatment — psychological ceiling. (3) Progress-bar animation on low-end Android can stutter — test on 2-year-old hardware, fall back to instant if frame budget exceeded. (4) Total step count visible from step 1 sets expectation — discovering '33 of 33' on screen 1 may scare users; alternative is to chunk and only reveal current section's count. (5) Cross-device resume (item 33): user on phone reaches step 8 of 12, opens iPad, sees... what? Progress hydration must persist exact step state.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: Progress indicators cross-culturally robust. RTL layouts (AR, HE): bar fills right-to-left (mirror direction); numeric counter reads naturally in localized format. JP/KR: subtler progress preferred (3pt bar, no celebration animations on bar fill — celebrations belong to milestones). LATAM: warmer color choices on fill (saffron, terracotta) outperform cold blue. CN: numeric step counter in Mandarin reads naturally; chunked section labels need locale-appropriate translation. Goal-gradient effect itself is cross-cultural (Kivetz replications across markets). Endowed-progress effect replicates in collectivist markets per limited cross-cultural research.
fields.prescribed_action: Show progress on every onboarding screen — top-of-screen 3px bar + numeric counter for short flows; chunked section sub-progress for >15-step flows. Apply endowed-progress: pre-fill bar 15-25% on Step 1 (visible_progress = (step+2)/(total+2) formula). Animate fill within 200ms of step transition (Doherty Threshold) using Reanimated 3 spring (damping:14, stiffness:140). Forbid indeterminate spinners during step transitions, equal-width segment gimmicks, percentage-only displays, indigo-500 default fill.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: Direct — every questionnaire screen carries the progress signal. Endowed-progress applies on Step 1 of the questionnaire. Chunked sub-progress maps to question-type clusters (preference section / demographic section / goal section / etc., item 27). Progress reaches 100% at the loader/AI-reveal step (N-1), disappears at paywall.
fields.aha_relevance: Goal-gradient acceleration toward the AI plan reveal compounds with sunk-cost — visible progress bar nearing 100% increases willingness to push through the final friction step. The 100%-fill micro-celebration at the loader-reveal hand-off is itself a peak moment (item 6, 53).
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: Progress treatments archetype-tunable: Hero (bold full-width bar, ascending fill animation, declarative '4 of 12' numeric counter), Sage (subtle hairline bar, Söhne Mono counter, no animation flourish), Caregiver (soft warm-color bar, gentle ease-in fill, gentle '4 of 12' label), Magician (subtle iridescent fill, near-invisible until completion when it pulses gold), Jester (segment-based with playful color rotation, slight overshoot on segment fill), Outlaw (raw 1px ink line, monospace counter, no celebration). Mismatched: Sage with bouncy spring overshoot reads juvenile; Caregiver with high-contrast acid green reads aggressive.
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against Wasted Loading Screens (the progress IS the loading — never indeterminate during step transitions). Pairs with item 13 (Personalized Plan Loader) — progress bar reaches 100% AT the loader, hands off to theatrical loader. Pairs against No-Escape Room (visible progress with affordance to back-navigate respects user agency).
sources: [Doherty & Thadhani (1982) — IBM Economic Value of Rapid Response](https://jlelliotton.blogspot.com/p/the-economic-value-of-rapid-response.html)
uncertain: specific RCT-grade conversion lift figures for endowed-progress in mobile onboarding (extrapolated from Kivetz car-wash context)
